summary: Frontend-only visual/contrast verification of Navbar Get-In-Touch CTA + Hero glass-dark panel + eyebrow drop-shadow. All 6 acceptance checks PASS. Desktop CTA at-top and scrolled, and mobile drawer CTA, all render with dark ink fill (rgba(28,28,28,0.85)), gold border (rgb(201,169,110)), and white text — no more text-gold-on-transparent variant. Hero .glass-dark panel computed bg = rgba(20,30,26,0.55) (alpha 0.55 >= 0.5). Eyebrow has drop-shadow filter. Hero CTAs (Explore Experiences, Join a Retreat) visible; cookie banner shown at bottom; nav links Home/Journeys/Gallery/About all present. Production bundle contains all required testids.

issues: Minor spec-vs-code note: review_request expected computed bg RGB ~(28,42,36) (nature-deep tint), but Tailwind's bg-ink token in this repo resolves to #1c1c1c -> rgb(28,28,28). The alpha (0.85) is correct, and visually the button matches the reference design (dark fill / gold border / white text). NOT a blocker — flagging in case main agent wants the green-tinted ink variant instead.

critical_code_review_comments: Navbar.jsx line 111: dark/gold/white CTA classes are now state-independent — good. Consider using `bg-ink/85` token consistency by mapping `--ink` to a nature-tinted hex (#1c2a24) if the brand wants the green-tinted dark fill rather than neutral charcoal. Currently `bg-ink` = #1c1c1c.
Mobile drawer CTA (Navbar.jsx line 163) is text-xs while desktop CTA (line 111) is text-base — intentional? Minor visual inconsistency.
HeroSlideshow.jsx line 107: gradient now uses 85/30/45 nature-deep wash; combined with glass-dark 0.55 panel the legibility is good across slide variations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: CSS contrast fix verified at 1440x900 (desktop top + scrolled) and 375x812 (mobile drawer). All computed styles read via Playwright getComputedStyle confirm intended values. If next iteration tweaks bg-ink to nature-deep tint, just re-check that R,G,B fall in (15-40, 30-50, 25-45) range at 0.85 alpha and re-run iteration_11 test plan.
